roadmap.goal: Align the team/portfolio creation HTTP gate and frontend gating with the inferred-rights model already implemented in RbacAdministrationService.CanCreateTeamAsync / CanCreatePortfolioAsync, and auto-grant the creator scoped-admin rights on the newly created entity.

validation.notes: Reviewer flagged 1 BLOCKER (Gherkin/table count mismatch — resolved by feature-delta edit splitting 14 Gherkin scenarios from 4 implementation-invariant tests), 2 HIGH (step-01-01 sizing: 14 tests in one step justified because they all turn from RED to GREEN on the same wiring fix — single root cause, single TDD cycle; 01-02→01-03 serial dependency: kept serial because the frontend should not trust a gate the backend has not yet been updated to enforce — deployment-order safety), and 1 LOW (01-04 test_file confusion — resolved by replacing test_file with a gate descriptor). Approval is conditional on these clarifications and the documented decisions stand for delivery.
blocker_findings_addressed: Feature-delta scenario table reconciled with .feature file: 14 Gherkin + 4 invariant tests; row IDs renumbered (1-14 Gherkin, TM-1 to TM-4 invariants).
accepted_high_findings: Step 01-01 sizing (14 tests in one cycle): kept as one step. Cohesion rationale: every RED test in CreateRightsAcceptanceTest.cs fails today for the same root cause (unimplemented switch arm OR throwing scaffold). A single coherent implementation flips all 14 to GREEN. Splitting would create an artificial RED-GREEN cycle whose first half cannot be exercised without the second.

Step 01-02→01-03 hard dependency: kept hard. Rationale: 01-03 changes the frontend to trust the new HTTP gate. Shipping 01-03 before 01-02 means the UI shows the Add button to Team Admins but the backend still returns 403 — broken UX in a release window.
low_findings_addressed: 01-04 test_file replaced with a gate descriptor; the actual gate is the full repository test suite remaining green.

out_of_scope: Full HTTP-level integration tests against the real RbacGuardAttribute pipeline. The existing TestWebApplicationFactory runs with Authentication.Enabled = false, which short-circuits IsRbacEnforcedAsync. Building a controllable test auth handler is documented in feature-delta Pre-requisites row 1 and is deferred to a follow-up feature.
Option B (dedicated TeamCreator / PortfolioCreator system roles). Tracked as an open question in feature-delta. Not blocking this delivery.

phases.name: Inferred-rights creation flow
phases.description: Wire the new RbacGuardRequirement values through the service, the HTTP controllers, and the frontend gating; auto-grant creator-admin on successful creation. Each step transitions a specific group of RED tests to GREEN.

steps.name: Service-layer inferred-rights and auto-admin grant (walking skeleton)
steps.description: Replace the scaffold throws with real implementations of GrantCreatorTeamAdminAsync and GrantCreatorPortfolioAdminAsync. Add two switch arms in CanSatisfyRequirementAsync delegating to the existing CanCreateTeamAsync / CanCreatePortfolioAsync methods. This step makes the walking skeleton scenario 'Team Admin creates a team and is recorded as its administrator' reachable end-to-end at the service level: inferred-rights guard evaluation plus observable UserPermission persistence in a real EF in-memory DbContext.
steps.scenario_name: Team Admin creates a team and is recorded as its administrator (walking skeleton) — covered by RbacGuard_CanCreateTeam_AdmitsTeamAdmin and GrantCreatorTeamAdminAsync_AfterTeamCreation_RecordsCreatorAsTeamAdminOfNewTeam
criteria: CanSatisfyRequirementAsync admits any user who holds at least one TeamAdmin role (direct or group-derived) when the requirement is CanCreateTeam.
CanSatisfyRequirementAsync admits any user who holds at least one PortfolioAdmin role (direct or group-derived) when the requirement is CanCreatePortfolio.
CanSatisfyRequirementAsync admits System Admins for both new requirement values regardless of scoped role state.
CanSatisfyRequirementAsync refuses callers who hold only Viewer roles for both new requirement values.
GrantCreatorTeamAdminAsync persists exactly one UserPermission row (Role=TeamAdmin, ScopeType=Team, ScopeId=teamId) for the given userProfileId.
GrantCreatorPortfolioAdminAsync persists exactly one UserPermission row (Role=PortfolioAdmin, ScopeType=Portfolio, ScopeId=portfolioId) for the given userProfileId.
Both grant methods are idempotent: calling them twice with identical arguments yields exactly one UserPermission row, not two.
Both grant methods preserve any pre-existing scoped admin permissions the creator already held; only the new (entity, role) tuple is added.
All 14 tests in CreateRightsAcceptanceTest.cs pass; the existing RbacAdministrationServiceTest suite remains green.
The // SCAFFOLD (feature: team-portfolio-creation-rights) and __SCAFFOLD__ markers are removed from the production source — grep returns zero matches under Lighthouse.Backend/.
out_of_scope: Auto-admin wiring from the controllers into these grant methods (handled in 01-02).

steps.name: Controller wiring: attribute swap, auto-admin call, obsolete-pin cleanup
steps.description: Swap the [RbacGuard] requirement on the four create/validate endpoints from SystemAdmin to the new CanCreate* values. Wire the auto-admin grant calls into TeamsController.CreateTeam and PortfoliosController.CreatePortfolio after a successful entity persistence, gated on IsRbacEnforcedAsync to avoid polluting permission rows in disabled-auth mode. Delete the two now-obsolete pin tests that asserted the previous SystemAdmin gate on these endpoints.
steps.scenario_name: CreateTeam controller uses the CanCreateTeam requirement (and symmetric scenarios for CreatePortfolio, ValidateTeamSettings, ValidatePortfolioSettings)
tests_to_delete: Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend.Tests/API/TeamsControllerTest.cs::CreateTeam_HasSystemAdminRbacGuardAttribute (lines ~530-541 — pin assumed the old SystemAdmin gate)
Lighthouse.Backend/Lighthouse.Backend.Tests/API/PortfoliosControllerTest.cs::CreatePortfolio_HasSystemAdminRbacGuardAttribute (lines ~348-359 — pin assumed the old SystemAdmin gate)
criteria: TeamsController.CreateTeam carries [RbacGuard(RbacGuardRequirement.CanCreateTeam)] (was SystemAdmin).
TeamsController.ValidateTeamSettings carries [RbacGuard(RbacGuardRequirement.CanCreateTeam)] (was SystemAdmin).
PortfoliosController.CreatePortfolio carries [RbacGuard(RbacGuardRequirement.CanCreatePortfolio)] (was SystemAdmin).
PortfoliosController.ValidatePortfolioSettings carries [RbacGuard(RbacGuardRequirement.CanCreatePortfolio)] (was SystemAdmin).

On successful CreateTeam, the controller resolves the current user via ICurrentUserProfileService.GetOrCreateFromPrincipalAsync and calls IRbacAdministrationService.GrantCreatorTeamAdminAsync with the newly created teamId, but only when IsRbacEnforcedAsync returns true.
On successful CreatePortfolio, the controller invokes the symmetric GrantCreatorPortfolioAdminAsync call under the same guard.
The two obsolete pin tests are deleted (not modified) — they assert behaviour that this feature deliberately changes; modifying them would dilute their original intent.
All four tests in CreateRightsControllerGuardTest.cs pass.
The existing TeamsControllerTest and PortfoliosControllerTest suites remain green aside from the two deletions called out above.
out_of_scope: Full HTTP-pipeline integration tests with a controllable auth handler (documented as a follow-up in feature-delta Pre-requisites).

steps.name: Frontend gating: route Add buttons through canCreateTeam / canCreatePortfolio
steps.description: In OverviewDashboard.tsx, replace the outer rbac.isSystemAdmin visibility guards on the Add Team and Add Portfolio buttons with rbac.canCreateTeam and rbac.canCreatePortfolio respectively. Keep the inner license-driven disabled logic. The 'connections required' precondition continues to apply only to System Admins (who own connection management), so non-System-Admin creators are not blocked by the absence of a connection they cannot configure. Update the OverviewDashboard tests that asserted System-Admin-only visibility to assert role-derived visibility instead, and roll back the rbac-ui-completeness D8 tightening.
steps.scenario_name: Add Team / Add Portfolio buttons visible to non-System-Admin creators (Pre-requisite row 2 + D8 rollback in feature-delta)
criteria: The Add Team button renders when rbac.canCreateTeam is true, regardless of rbac.isSystemAdmin.
The Add Portfolio button renders when rbac.canCreatePortfolio is true, regardless of rbac.isSystemAdmin.
The connections-required precondition on the Add Team button applies only when the current user is a System Admin; a Team Admin without configured connections can still see and use the button.
License-driven disabled behaviour (canCreateTeam / canCreatePortfolio from useLicenseRestrictions) is preserved unchanged.

Existing OverviewDashboard tests that asserted 'System-Admin-only visibility' for the Add buttons are updated to assert the new role-derived visibility contract — not deleted en masse, but rewritten in place so the test still covers the intent (gating by capability, not by SystemAdmin).
pnpm test, pnpm build (which runs tsc -b and Biome via the prebuild hook) all pass with zero warnings.
No new SonarQube issues introduced (cognitive complexity, duplication, any-in-TS, etc).
out_of_scope: Changes to useRbac() — the hook already exposes canCreateTeam / canCreatePortfolio derived from /my-summary; no signature change needed.
Changes to the OnboardingStepper visibility — it remains a System-Admin-only onboarding aid.

steps.name: Back-propagate annotations to superseded feature deltas
steps.description: Doc-only step. Annotate the prior feature deltas whose decisions this feature overrides or reverts, so the project's wave history stays coherent and future readers can trace the chain.
steps.scenario_name: No new behavioural scenario — gate is full-suite green after doc-only edits
criteria: rbac-enhancements/feature-delta.md WD-03 row carries an in-place annotation 'Superseded 2026-05-11 by team-portfolio-creation-rights — inferred-rights model in RbacAdministrationService.CanCreateTeamAsync / CanCreatePortfolioAsync is the service-level truth; this feature aligns the HTTP gate and frontend gating with it.'
rbac-enhancements/feature-delta.md Q4 row carries the symmetric annotation.

rbac-ui-completeness/feature-delta.md D8 row carries the annotation 'Reverted 2026-05-11 by team-portfolio-creation-rights — frontend gating now uses rbac.canCreateTeam / rbac.canCreatePortfolio directly, not AND-with-isSystemAdmin.'
No production source files touched; the full pnpm test + dotnet test suites remain green.
Annotations are inline next to the affected rows (not appended in a separate section) so future readers see them at the point of decision.
out_of_scope: Restructuring or summarising the superseded feature deltas — only the targeted rows get an annotation.
steps.gate: full repository test suite remains green (dotnet test + pnpm test)

walking_skeleton.scenario: Team Admin creates a team and is recorded as its administrator
walking_skeleton.coverage_estimate: 100% of the @walking_skeleton scenario at the service level. The HTTP-pipeline portion of the WS is deliberately deferred (feature-delta Pre-requisites row 1) and remains a documented follow-up; the service-level coverage proves the inferred-rights guard evaluation and the auto-admin persistence — the two behaviours the bug report names directly.

scaffold_removal_check.expected_after_01_02: zero matches (controller wiring does not add scaffolds)

backend: dotnet test — all xUnit/NUnit suites green, including CreateRightsAcceptanceTest and CreateRightsControllerGuardTest
frontend: pnpm test — all Vitest suites green
pnpm build — tsc -b plus vite build complete with zero errors and zero warnings (Biome runs as prebuild)
sonarqube: No new issues introduced (bugs, vulnerabilities, code smells, security hotspots)
